(born 6 June 1983) is an Estonian [long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_runner "Long-distance runner") who specialises in the [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He competed at the [2015 World Championships](/wiki/2015_World_Championships_in_Athletics "2015 World Championships in Athletics") in Beijing finishing 20th, the [2016 Olympic Games](/wiki/2016_Summer_Olympics "2016 Summer Olympics") in Rio de Janeiro finishing 61st, and the [2017 World Championships](/wiki/2017_World_Championships_in_Athletics "2017 World Championships in Athletics") in London finishing 53rd. He previously competed mostly in [middle-distance](/wiki/Middle-distance_running "Middle-distance running") events switching to the marathon in 2013.[[1]](#cite_note-EC-1) He won the [Tallinn Marathon](/wiki/Tallinn_Marathon "Tallinn Marathon") in 2018.
* [800 metres](/wiki/800_metres "800 metres") – 1:49.07 (Dessau 2005)
* [1500 metres](/wiki/1500_metres "1500 metres") – 3:48.72 (Tartu 2012)
["Roman Fosti"](http://www.iaaf.org/athletes/estonia/roman-fosti-190046). IAAF. 22 August 2015. Retrieved 22 August 2015.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Estonian long-distance runner

Roman Fosti

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Fosti at the [2018 European Championships](/wiki/2018_European_Athletics_Championships "2018 European Athletics Championships") | |
| Personal information | |
| Born | (1983-06-06) 6 June 1983 (age 42) |
| Height | 1.81 m (5 ft 11 in)[[1]](#cite_note-EC-1) |
| Weight | 70 kg (154 lb) |
| Sport | |
| Sport | [Athletics](/wiki/Athletics_(sport) "Athletics (sport)") |
| Event(s) | 800 m, 1500 m, half marathon, marathon |
| Club | Stamina SK[[1]](#cite_note-EC-1) |
| Coached by | [Jane Salumäe](/wiki/Jane_Salum%C3%A4e "Jane Salumäe")[[1]](#cite_note-EC-1) |

**Roman Fosti** (born 6 June 1983) is an Estonian [long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_runner "Long-distance runner") who specialises in the [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He competed at the [2015 World Championships](/wiki/2015_World_Championships_in_Athletics "2015 World Championships in Athletics") in Beijing finishing 20th, the [2016 Olympic Games](/wiki/2016_Summer_Olympics "2016 Summer Olympics") in Rio de Janeiro finishing 61st, and the [2017 World Championships](/wiki/2017_World_Championships_in_Athletics "2017 World Championships in Athletics") in London finishing 53rd. He previously competed mostly in [middle-distance](/wiki/Middle-distance_running "Middle-distance running") events switching to the marathon in 2013.[[1]](#cite_note-EC-1) He won the [Tallinn Marathon](/wiki/Tallinn_Marathon "Tallinn Marathon") in 2018.
